scope: This course will prepare the young pharmacy student to interact effectively with doctors, nurses, dentists, physiotherapists and other health workers. At the end of this course the student will get the soft skills set to work cohesively with the team as a team player and will add value to the pharmaceutical business.
objectives: Understand the behavioral needs for a Pharmacist to function effectively in the areas of pharmaceutical operation.
Effectively manage the team as a team player.
courseOutcomes: CO1: Understand communication process, barriers, and perspectives.
CO2: Master elements of face-to-face communication and communication styles.
CO3: Demonstrate basic listening skills and effective written communication.
CO4: Develop interview skills and presentation delivery.
CO5: Participate effectively in group discussions.

scope: The learning modules are to be conducted using wordsworth® English language lab software.
objectives: Gain practical experience in presentation, interview and group discussion.
courseOutcomes: CO1: Express basic communication topics such as meeting people, asking questions, making friends.
CO2: Pronounce consonant sounds, vowels, and nouns in English accurately.
CO3: Demonstrate advanced comprehension, figures of speech and listening skills.
CO4: Illustrate effective writing and email etiquette.
CO5: Execute structured presentations, interview handling and interview skills.

scope: To learn and understand the components of living world, structure and functional system of plant and animal kingdom.
objectives: Know the classification and salient features of five kingdoms of life.
Understand the basic components of anatomy and physiology of plant.
Know and understand the basic components of anatomy and physiology animal with special reference to human.
courseOutcomes: CO1: Classify the living world, name organisms, and understand morphology/anatomy of flowering plants.
CO2: Discuss body fluids, human circulatory system, digestion, and absorption mechanisms.
CO3: Explain breathing, respiration, excretion, neural coordination, chemical regulation, and reproduction.
CO4: Understand plant nutrition, nitrogen metabolism, and photosynthesis.
CO5: Discuss plant respiration, growth regulators, cell structures, and animal/plant tissues.

scope: Compounding and identification tests using computer models, histological slides, and physiological measurements.
objectives: Examine microstructures and slide preparations under a microscope.
courseOutcomes: CO1: Use microscope and perform section cutting, mounting, staining, and permanent slide preparation.
CO2: Study cell structure and its inclusions.
CO3: Study stem, root, leaf structures and their modifications.
CO4: Understand the structure and identification of bones and tissues.
CO5: Determine blood group, blood pressure, and tidal volume.

recommendedBooks.author: S.B.Gokhale, C.K.Kokate and S.P.Shriwastava
recommendedBooks.title: A Manual of pharmaceutical biology practical

scope: This is an introductory course in mathematics. This subject deals with the introduction to Partial fraction, Logarithm, matrices and Determinant, Analytical geometry, Calculus, differential equation and Laplace transform.
objectives: Know the theory and their application in Pharmacy.
Solve the different types of problems by applying theory.
Appreciate the important application of mathematics in Pharmacy.
courseOutcomes: CO1: Resolve algebraic expressions using partial fractions and logarithms.
CO2: Define, classify, and solve functions, limits, and continuity equations.
CO3: Solve systems of linear equations using matrices and determinants.
CO4: Apply differential and integral calculus equations to pharmaceutical kinetics.
CO5: Formulate and solve differential equations and Laplace transforms.

units.name: Unit I
units.unitName: Partial fraction, Logarithms, Function & Limits and continuity
units.title: Partial fraction, Logarithms, Function & Limits and continuity
units.description: Partial fraction, Logarithms, Function & Limits and continuity

topics.topicName: Partial fraction: Introduction, Polynomial, Rational fractions, Proper and Improper fractions, Partial fraction, Resolving into Partial fraction, Application of Partial Fraction in Chemical Kinetics and Pharmacokinetics.
topics.name: Partial fraction: Introduction, Polynomial, Rational fractions, Proper and Improper fractions, Partial fraction, Resolving into Partial fraction, Application of Partial Fraction in Chemical Kinetics and Pharmacokinetics.

topics.topicName: Logarithms: Introduction, Definition, Theorems/Properties of logarithms, Common logarithms, Characteristic and Mantissa, worked examples, application of logarithm to solve pharmaceutical problems.
topics.name: Logarithms: Introduction, Definition, Theorems/Properties of logarithms, Common logarithms, Characteristic and Mantissa, worked examples, application of logarithm to solve pharmaceutical problems.

topics.topicName: Function: Real Valued function, Classification of real valued functions.
topics.name: Function: Real Valued function, Classification of real valued functions.

topics.topicName: Limits and continuity: Introduction, Limit of a function, Definition of limit of a function (ε - δ definition), standard limits and limit properties.
topics.name: Limits and continuity: Introduction, Limit of a function, Definition of limit of a function (ε - δ definition), standard limits and limit properties.

topics.topicName: Matrices and Determinant: Introduction matrices, Types of matrices, Operation on matrices, Transpose of a matrix, Matrix Multiplication, Determinants, Properties of determinants, Product of determinants, Minors and co-Factors, Adjoint or adjugate of a square matrix, Singular and non-singular matrices, Inverse of a matrix, Solution of system of linear of equations using matrix method, Cramer‘s rule, Characteristic equation and roots of a square matrix, Cayley–Hamilton theorem, Application of Matrices in solving Pharmacokinetic equations.
topics.name: Matrices and Determinant: Introduction matrices, Types of matrices, Operation on matrices, Transpose of a matrix, Matrix Multiplication, Determinants, Properties of determinants, Product of determinants, Minors and co-Factors, Adjoint or adjugate of a square matrix, Singular and non-singular matrices, Inverse of a matrix, Solution of system of linear of equations using matrix method, Cramer‘s rule, Characteristic equation and roots of a square matrix, Cayley–Hamilton theorem, Application of Matrices in solving Pharmacokinetic equations.

topics.topicName: Calculus: Differentiation: Introductions, Derivative of a function, Derivative of a constant, Derivative of a product of a constant and a function, Derivative of the sum or difference of two functions, Derivative of the product of two functions (product formula), Derivative of the quotient of two functions (Quotient formula) – Without Proof, Derivative of x^n w.r.t x, where n is any rational number, Derivative of e^x, Derivative of log_e x, Derivative of a^x, Derivative of trigonometric functions from first principles (without Proof), Successive Differentiation, Conditions for a function to be a maximum or a minimum at a point. Application.
topics.name: Calculus: Differentiation: Introductions, Derivative of a function, Derivative of a constant, Derivative of a product of a constant and a function, Derivative of the sum or difference of two functions, Derivative of the product of two functions (product formula), Derivative of the quotient of two functions (Quotient formula) – Without Proof, Derivative of x^n w.r.t x, where n is any rational number, Derivative of e^x, Derivative of log_e x, Derivative of a^x, Derivative of trigonometric functions from first principles (without Proof), Successive Differentiation, Conditions for a function to be a maximum or a minimum at a point. Application.
